Growth hormone peptides. Hormone optimization. Metformin, dihydroberberine, Jardiance, or alpha lipoic acid for the insulin pathway. Survodutide vs tirzepatide vs retatrutide. Survodutide is retatrutide minus the GIP component (GLP plus glucagon). I don't see the point unless price wins. top_sources.top_context: ou have to take GLPs forever? Think blast and cruise. Blast to lose the weight, then cruise on a microdose for 8 to 9 months of the year. Take a month or two off here and there. You don't want to be on 8 mg a week forever. If Reta becomes unavailable. I don't see it disappearing. But mazdutide and survodutide paired with tirzepatide would give you GLP, GIP, and glucagon agonism. Similar results. reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the prescribing physician to consider Hunter Williams's 2026-03-31 framing: "Survodutide and mazdutide are not retatrutide. But they're not far off. And they fill important gaps that tirzepatide and semaglutide can't address." [Hunter Williams, email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, 2026-03-31, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB] Hunter Williams also says, "People who escalate every 4 weeks instead of every 2 weeks are far more compliant and much better able to stay on the compound long term. Don't rush the titration." [same source]
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-31): "Survodutide (rougher Phase 2) - 66% nausea, 49% diarrhea, 41% vomiting - 20-24% discontinuation during rapid bi-weekly dose escalation." [email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB]
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-31): "Phase 2 aggressive bi-weekly escalation caused dropouts" and "Phase 3 = 4-week titration intervals + dose flexibility + pause/restart option." [email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB]
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-31): "Likely need to scale down Reta dose before introducing either of these" and "Same GLP-1 pathway but glucagon agonism feels different from GIP." [email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB]
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-30): "you don' want be on eight milligrams a week forever is what I'd say." [library video slug peptide-qa-february-2026--cba19117, 18:20, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/peptide-qa-february-2026--cba19117/]
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-31): "NOT approved anywhere yet." [email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB]
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Hunter Williams's older library article says, "Survodutide is retatrutide minus the GIP component (GLP plus glucagon). I don't see the point unless price wins." [library article slug peptide-qa-march-2025--e47049f7, 2026-03-29, no transcript cue timestamp available, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/peptide-qa-march-2025--e47049f7/] Hunter Williams's newer 2026-03-31 email says, "Survodutide and mazdutide are not retatrutide. But they're not far off. And they fill important gaps that tirzepatide and semaglutide can't address." [email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB] Hunter Williams's 2026-03-30 library video says, "cervidutide and mazutite look great. Those are basically a GLP plus a glucagon agonist, so they don't use the GIP receptor." [library video slug peptide-qa-february-2026--cba19117, 18:52, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/peptide-qa-february-2026--cba19117/]

chronic.dose: "1-2 mg/wk to begin (either compound)"; "Phase 3 target: 3.6 mg + 6.0 mg"; "Survodutide | 4.8 mg/wk | GLP-1 + Glucagon | 46 wk | ~18.7% | ~67% (>30% ↓)"; "Example: 6 mg/wk → 2 mg Mon + 2 mg Wed + 2 mg Fri." [Hunter Williams, 2026-03-31, email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB]
chronic.frequency: "Once weekly" and "Always prefer to split weekly total across 2-3 injections rather than all at once." [Hunter Williams, 2026-03-31, email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB]
chronic.duration: "Minimum 4 weeks at each dose before moving up" and "Phase 3 = 4-week titration intervals + dose flexibility + pause/restart option." [Hunter Williams, 2026-03-31, email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7, https://open.spotify.com/episode/2IADOstRyI6FrZlNHriPJB]
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter Williams (2026-03-31), email threadId 19d45fc334be87a7: "1-2 mg/wk to begin (either compound) - See response, scale from there - Minimum 4 weeks at each dose before moving up."
